name: Clarithromycin
executiveSummary: Macrolide. Treatment of upper and lower respiratory tract infections, acute otitis media due to Mycoplasma and Chlamydia. Prophylaxis and treatment of Mycobacterium avium infections.
indications:
## Main indications

Macrolide. Treatment of upper and lower respiratory tract infections, acute otitis media due to Mycoplasma and Chlamydia. Prophylaxis and treatment of Mycobacterium avium infections.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Lyophilized powder vial containing 500 mg (Claritromicina Northia, Pharmavial, Richet, Kiaricid).
adulto.diluent: 500 mg in 250 mL 0.9% NaCl or 5% dextrose.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: No. Intermittent IV: Yes. Reconstitute 500 mg with 10 mL SWFI, dilute in 250 mL 0.9% NaCl, 5% dextrose and administer over 60 min.
adulto.notes: Use only SWFI for reconstitution, as other diluents may cause precipitation. Do not use 0.9% NaCl. Irritant (may cause phlebitis).

pediatrico.dose: Children: 15 mg/kg/day every 12 h, adolescents and adults (PO): 500 mg every 12 h. Antituberculous: 30 mg/kg/day every 12 h, maximum dose: 1 g/day.
pediatrico.presentation: Tablets: 250 mg; Suspension: 25 mg/mL; Powder vial: 500 mg
pediatrico.notes: Contraindicated in combination with terfenadine in patients with cardiac disease. Interactions: carbamazepine, cisapride, rifabutin. Do not refrigerate reconstituted suspension.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 24 h at room temperature and 48 h refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- 6 h at room temperature and 48 h refrigerated.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Diarrhea, nausea, abnormal taste, dyspepsia, abdominal pain, headaches, hypersensitivity. See alert.
